level_signals: 5+ years in Technical Program Management or similar roles
Partners with engineering leaders and executives as a strategic thought partner
Owns cross-functional programs spanning GPU allocation, inference cost, and R&D spend

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years in Technical Program Management or similar roles in highly technical environments.
Experience with cloud infrastructure costs, GPU/compute capacity planning, or COGs/R&D attribution.
Strong analytical skills; comfortable with cost data, utilization metrics, and financial models.
Proven ability to drive cross-functional programs across Engineering, ML, and Finance without direct authority.
Bias toward action and outcomes over process and ceremony.

scope.surface: Infrastructure cost attribution, GPU/compute capacity planning, and R&D spend efficiency across ML and Infrastructure teams
primary_metrics_inferred: GPU and infrastructure allocation aligned to business priorities
Cost optimization program outcomes on predictable cadence
core_responsibilities: Own COGs and R&D attribution programs across inference, compute, and infrastructure workloads
Drive GPU and infrastructure resource allocation frameworks and recurring reviews
Manage technical programs in cost optimization, capacity planning, reliability, and migration
Partner strategically with engineering leaders on resource tradeoffs and investment prioritization
Dig into dashboards, data, and systems; write docs, build models, run analyses

green_flags: Very specific scope (COGs, GPU allocation, R&D efficiency) — not a vague TPM role
Small, flat, talent-dense organization signals high individual impact
Sample projects section gives concrete examples of what success looks like
Role has clear executive/engineering sponsor model indicating organizational support
red_flags: No location, compensation, or visa sponsorship information provided anywhere in the JD
No mention of remote eligibility or office requirements — unclear work arrangement
No equity or benefits details disclosed
